questions.questionText: Who sang the hit song 'Thriller'?
What was the first music video played on MTV when it launched on August 1, 1981?
options: Video Killed the Radio Star by The Buggles
Hungry Like the Wolf by Duran Duran
questions.correctAnswer: Video Killed the Radio Star by The Buggles

questions.questionText: Which Madonna song was her first number-one hit on the Billboard Hot 100?
Which band performed 'Livin' on a Prayer'?
What year did MTV launch?
Which artist recorded the 1984 hit 'When Doves Cry'?
Cyndi Lauper's 'Girls Just Want to Have Fun' was released from which debut album?
Which band had a hit with 'Sweet Dreams (Are Made of This)' in 1983?
The Live Aid concerts in 1985 were held to raise money for famine relief in which country?
'Born in the U.S.A.' is an album by which artist?
Which Guns N' Roses song was their only number-one single on the Billboard Hot 100?
Which Wham! single, written by George Michael, was the duo's first UK and US number-one hit?
options: Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go
questions.correctAnswer: Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go

questions.questionText: Which Norwegian band had a worldwide hit with 'Take On Me' in 1985?
Who performed 'What's Love Got to Do with It', which reached number one in the US in 1984?
What is the name of U2's 1987 album that featured 'With or Without You'?
'Every Breath You Take' was a 1983 hit by which band?
Which Van Halen song, featuring a prominent synthesizer riff, was their only number-one hit on the Billboard Hot 100?
Whitney Houston had seven consecutive US number-one singles in the 1980s.
Which synth-pop duo had a number-one hit with 'West End Girls' in 1986?
Which 1985 charity single was written by Michael Jackson and Lionel Richie and performed by USA for Africa?
Who produced Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' album?
Which Tears for Fears song topped the US Billboard Hot 100 and was described by the band as being about the desire for control and power?
options: Everybody Wants to Rule the World
questions.correctAnswer: Everybody Wants to Rule the World

questions.questionText: The Run-DMC and Aerosmith collaboration 'Walk This Way' appeared on which Run-DMC album?
Which Def Leppard album, produced by Robert John 'Mutt' Lange, featured the hit 'Pour Some Sugar on Me'?
New Order's 'Blue Monday', released in 1983, holds what distinction in music history?
options: First single to use a drum machine

questions.questionText: New Order was formed by the surviving members of which post-punk band after the death of their lead singer Ian Curtis?
Nena's '99 Luftballons' reached number two on the US Billboard Hot 100. In which language was the US hit version sung?
Which hip-hop producer brought Run-DMC and Aerosmith together for the 'Walk This Way' collaboration in 1986?
Which Duran Duran song reached number 3 on the Billboard Hot 100 after heavy rotation of its music video on MTV?
The Live Aid concerts on July 13, 1985 were held simultaneously at Wembley Stadium in London and which US venue?
options: Madison Square Garden in New York
John F. Kennedy Stadium in Philadelphia
questions.correctAnswer: John F. Kennedy Stadium in Philadelphia

questions.questionText: Phil Collins' debut solo single 'In the Air Tonight' was released in 1981 from which album?
Which Talking Heads concert film, directed by Jonathan Demme and released in 1984, is widely considered one of the greatest concert movies ever made?
Metallica's 'Master of Puppets' (1986) is notable for being the first album of which genre to be certified platinum?
The charity song 'Do They Know It's Christmas?' (1984) was written by Bob Geldof and which Ultravox member?
Which Dire Straits song featured Sting singing the opening line 'I want my MTV' and was taken from the 1985 album 'Brothers in Arms'?
The Smiths, featuring vocalist Morrissey and guitarist Johnny Marr, were formed in 1982 in which English city?
Which George Michael solo album, released in 1987, became the first album by a white solo artist to reach number one on the Billboard Top Black Albums chart?
Which INXS single from the 1987 album 'Kick' was the Australian band's only US number-one hit?
R.E.M. was formed in 1980 in Athens, Georgia. 'It's the End of the World as We Know It (And I Feel Fine)' appeared on which 1987 album?
Bruce Springsteen's 'Born in the U.S.A.' album (1984) produced how many top-10 singles on the Billboard Hot 100?
How many singles from Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' album reached the top 10 on the Billboard Hot 100?
The music video for Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' was directed by John Landis. Approximately how long is the video?
Prince's 'When Doves Cry' (1984) was notable for lacking which instrument that is normally present in pop and R&B recordings?
Which Bon Jovi album, produced by Bruce Fairbairn and released in 1986, was named by Billboard as the top-selling album of 1987?
Def Leppard drummer Rick Allen lost his left arm in a car accident on New Year's Eve of which year?
Cyndi Lauper's debut album 'She's So Unusual' (1983) was the first debut album by a female artist to achieve how many top-five singles on the Billboard Hot 100?

Whitney Houston's self-titled debut album (1985) set a record for a female artist in the 1980s by accumulating how many total weeks at number one on the Billboard 200 (combined with her second album 'Whitney')?
The Cure's 'Just Like Heaven' (1987) appeared on which album?
options: Kiss Me, Kiss Me, Kiss Me
questions.correctAnswer: Kiss Me, Kiss Me, Kiss Me

questions.questionText: Mark Knopfler of Dire Straits wrote the lyrics to 'Money for Nothing' after overhearing workers complaining while watching MTV in what type of store?
Guns N' Roses' debut album 'Appetite for Destruction' (1987) has been certified how many times platinum by the RIAA?
Depeche Mode's 'Enjoy the Silence' was originally written by Martin Gore as what type of song before being reworked by the band?
Metallica's 'Master of Puppets' (1986) was the band's final album to feature which bassist, who died in a tour bus accident later that year?
'Girls Just Want to Have Fun' was originally written and recorded as a demo in 1979 by which songwriter before Cyndi Lauper made it famous?
Soft Cell's 'Tainted Love' (1981) was a cover of a song originally recorded in 1964 by which artist?
The English lyrics for Nena's '99 Red Balloons' were written by Kevin McAlea, an Irish musician best known for working with which other artist?
Roxette's 'The Look' became a US number-one hit in 1989 after an American exchange student brought a copy of their album home to which US state?
Prince became the first North American artist to simultaneously have a number-one album, movie, and single in 1984. What was the number-one single?
Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' album spent how many non-consecutive weeks at number one on the Billboard 200?
Which producer was originally slated to produce Def Leppard's 'Hysteria' album before Robert John 'Mutt' Lange ultimately took over?
Tina Turner's 'What's Love Got to Do with It' (1984) was written by which songwriting duo?
